generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance OccupancyControlActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Automated actuation of building occupancy control systems via digital twin-driven protocols for real-time dynamic adjustment of HVAC zoning and lighting based on occupancy data"
]

instance HVACOptimizationActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Model-driven tuning of HVAC systems for energy efficiency optimization through predictive analytics and real-time control adjustments"
]

instance SmartLightingActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Automated actuation of building lighting systems using digital twin-driven protocols for occupancy-based dynamic lighting control"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental parameter tracking of temperature via IoT sensors in academic buildings and libraries"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of humidity levels via IoT sensors for indoor environmental condition tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataStream
]

instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time measurement of CO₂ levels via IoT sensors for air quality monitoring in academic spaces"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataStream
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection via IoT sensors for occupancy forecasting and resource allocation optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataStream
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption profiling for HVAC and lighting optimization in academic buildings"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataStream
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring for critical infrastructure failure prediction in academic buildings"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataStream
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance CO2DataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
]

instance OccupancyDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance StructuralHealthDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitor
]

// C10: Models
instance LibraryEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D spatial modeling of indoor library environments correlating physical and digital representations for occupancy and environmental condition analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMIntegrationFramework
]

instance BuildingPerformanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model-driven simulation of building performance metrics including energy efficiency and occupant comfort validation for LEED certification"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelPredictiveControlFramework
]

instance OccupancyPredictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics model for occupancy forecasting in library spaces to optimize resource allocation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelPredictiveControlFramework
]

instance HVACOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model predictive control framework for dynamic HVAC system optimization based on real-time environmental data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelPredictiveControlFramework
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin data management platform for event streaming, versioning, and metadata handling with real-time synchronization capabilities"
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "IoT connectivity platform enabling seamless integration with sensors and edge devices via MQTT protocol for real-time data acquisition"
]

instance UnrealEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization engine for interactive simulations and augmented reality overlays of building environments"
]

instance ApacheKafka : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Distributed event store for real-time data ingestion and time-series storage in the digital twin architecture"
]

instance Neo4jGraphDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Graph database for relationship-centric modeling of building components and their interactions in the digital twin system"
]

instance ModelPredictiveControlFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model predictive control libraries for optimization-driven actuation of building systems based on real-time data"
]

instance BACnetProtocolStack : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Building Automation and Control Network protocol stack for interoperability between physical sensors and digital systems"
]

instance BIMIntegrationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven framework for BIM-based device modeling and semantic enrichment of building components in the digital twin"
]

// C12: Data
instance RealTimeEnvironmentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental data stream combining temperature, humidity, CO₂ levels, and occupancy information from IoT sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream, HumidityDataStream, CO2DataStream, OccupancyDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BuildingPerformanceModel, OccupancyPredictionModel
]

instance EnergyPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Energy consumption data stream for HVAC and lighting systems optimization across academic buildings"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HVACOptimizationModel
]

instance StructuralHealthMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring metrics for critical infrastructure failure prediction in academic buildings"
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralHealthDataStream
]

instance SpatialTemporalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "3D spatial-temporal data representation of indoor environments for visualization and analysis in library digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream, HumidityDataStream, OccupancyDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LibraryEnvironmentModel
]

// C6: Services
instance EnvironmentalMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental parameter tracking including temperature, humidity, CO₂ levels, and occupancy via IoT sensors"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeEnvironmentInsights, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance OccupancyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Occupancy forecasting and resource allocation optimization for library spaces using predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:enables OccupancyForecasting, ResourceAllocationService
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Energy consumption profiling and HVAC/lighting system optimization for academic buildings"
    DTDFVocab:enables EnergyProfileAnalysis, HVACOptimization
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Failure prediction for critical infrastructure (e.g., HVAC systems) using historical and real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:enables FailurePrediction, MaintenanceScheduling
]

instance ControlActuationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated actuation of building systems including smart thermostats and automated lighting via digital twin protocols"
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACControl, LightingControl
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboards and 3D spatial modeling for researchers/operators to visualize spatial-temporal data"
    DTDFVocab:enables SpatialTemporalVisualization
]

instance LEEDComplianceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated reporting for LEED certification audits through digital twin data integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComplianceReporting
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeEnvironmentInsights : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of real-time environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, CO₂, occupancy) for operational anomaly detection"
]

instance OccupancyForecasting : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics output for occupancy patterns in library spaces to optimize resource allocation"
]

instance EnergyProfileAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Energy consumption profiling results showing HVAC and lighting system performance metrics"
]

instance HVACOptimization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of HVAC zoning and ventilation rates based on real-time occupancy and environmental data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LightingControl : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of building lighting levels based on occupancy predictions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailurePrediction : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of potential infrastructure failures (e.g., HVAC system malfunctions) using historical and real-time data"
]

instance MaintenanceScheduling : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote diagnostics and maintenance scheduling based on digital twin failure predictions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpatialTemporalVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of indoor environmental conditions showing spatial-temporal data correlations"
]

instance ComplianceReporting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of LEED certification compliance reports from digital twin data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of sensor drift or actuator failures in the building systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ResourceAllocationService : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic allocation of resources (lighting, seating) based on occupancy predictions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C13: Environment
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, CO2Sensor,
    OccupancySensor, EnergyConsumptionSensor,
    HVACOptimizationActuator, SmartLightingActuator,
    LibraryEnvironmentModel, BuildingPerformanceModel
]

instance ResearchLibraryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains OccupancySensor, StructuralHealthMonitor,
    OccupancyPredictionModel, VisualizationService,
    LEEDComplianceService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The digital twin facilitates **adaptive control** for physical infrastructure via **Facility Managers**, who interact through bidirectional communication channels. Command types include **operational adjustments** (e.g., adaptive lighting systems as referenced in related projects) and control messages formatted via the DT Framework’s **centralized hub**. Validation and authentication mechanisms are implicitly enforced through the framework’s **interoperability requirements**, ensuring secure and conflict-free execution. Feedback systems loop back operational data to refine digital models dynamically, with confirmation of command execution implicitly validated via real-time synchronization. No explicit mention of HVAC-specific commands or conflict resolution is documented, but the framework’s modularity supports extensibility for such use cases.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs **real-time data exchange capabilities** for continuous synchronization between physical building components and their digital counterparts, ensuring minimal latency for operational monitoring and control. The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, enabling adaptive synchronization frequencies and adjustments to temporal granularity without requiring full redeployment. While the documents do not specify exact latency metrics (e.g., sub-second requirements), the emphasis on real-time synchronization and dynamic operational needs implies stringent temporal alignment demands, critical for AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, Operations) workflows where immediate feedback is essential for decision-making and maintenance optimization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **role-based orchestration model** where the **DT Framework** serves as the central orchestrator, facilitating **coordinated roles** (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) across the life cycle. The architecture leverages **modular components** with standardized extension points for service integration, enabling adaptability to evolving operational needs. **Resource management** is implicit through runtime configuration, allowing dynamic adjustments without redeployment. The system emphasizes **holistic building life cycle management** by integrating enriched BIM models into operational DTs, with explicit emphasis on **bidirectional data exchange** between physical and digital domains. The conceptual model (Figure 1) illustrates a **coordinated flow** from BIM enrichment to deployment, emphasizing synchronized roles and system components for seamless operation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle stages of the Digital Twin (DT) system span from **initial device modeling to custom service development**, structured around three core phases aligned with the **model-driven deployment framework**:

1. **Device Modeling and BIM Enrichment (Phase 1)**
   - **Representation Type**: Enriched **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** models, augmented with **Building Control System (BCS) components** (sensors, actuators, controllers) to address BIM’s static limitations.
   - **Transition Management**: Manual configuration by **Building Control Engineers** via BIM tools, followed by **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles to extract structured representations.
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Enables **unified semantic foundations** for subsequent transformation steps, ensuring compatibility with dynamic DT requirements.

2. **DT Artifact Generation and Deployment (Phase 2)**
   - **Representation Type**: Automatically generated **deployable DT artifacts** from enriched BIM models via MDE, minimizing manual intervention.
   - **Transition Management**: Real-time synchronization between physical infrastructure and digital twin via **continuous data exchange mechanisms** (implied by 'real-time data exchange capabilities').
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Supports **modular architecture** with standardized extension points for integrating custom services (e.g., plugins, analytics).

3. **Runtime Operation and Evolution (Phase 3)**
   - **Representation Type**: Dynamic, **interactive visualization interfaces** for **Facility Managers** to monitor and control operations, alongside **custom DT services** developed by **DT Engineers**.
   - **Transition Management**: Continuous updates via **runtime synchronization** and **extension points** for evolving operational requirements.
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Governed by **version control** (implied for custom services) and **quality assurance practices** during deployment (e.g., automated artifact validation).

**Key Transition Mechanisms**:
- **From BIM to DT Artifacts**: MDE-driven transformation leveraging BIM’s structured data.
- **Between Phases**: Real-time data pipelines and modular service integration ensure seamless evolution.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
    The DT system prioritizes **model fidelity** through **BIM-based object-oriented models**, which inherently capture high-resolution geometric and functional attributes of building components. Validation and verification are achieved via **model-driven automation frameworks** that reduce manual intervention (C2), ensuring reproducibility and consistency in DT deployments. The system’s real-time synchronization (C3) minimizes latency and drift between physical and digital states, while the modular architecture (C4) supports extensibility for integrating advanced validation tools (e.g., simulation plugins, IoT data validation layers). However, the documents do not explicitly detail uncertainty quantification, error detection mechanisms, or quality assurance processes for models/data beyond the emphasis on **semantic integration, real-time connectivity, and extensibility**.
    "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin Framework operates as a **centralized hub** with a **modular architecture**, leveraging **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** for real-time data exchange. The network topology is not explicitly detailed, but the system supports **scalable, heterogeneous interoperability** via standardized extension points. While specific protocols like OPC UA or TLS 1.3 are **not mentioned**, the framework’s emphasis on **low-overhead updates** and **runtime adaptability** implies a robust, fault-tolerant infrastructure. Security protocols are implied through the **interoperability and maintainability requirements**, but no explicit details (e.g., encryption standards) are provided.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin deployment follows a **model-driven, scalable approach** without specifying cloud/edge/on-premise hosting. The framework supports **modular, low-overhead updates** and **runtime configuration management**, enabling incremental evolution without full redeployment. Deployment models are abstracted via the **central hub architecture**, with scalability implied by the system’s ability to handle entire infrastructure systems. Availability/reliability is addressed through **maintainability requirements**, but no explicit metrics (e.g., uptime SLAs) or hardware specifications (e.g., computational/storage requirements) are documented.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems via standardized extension points** and **interoperability standards** to ensure compatibility with heterogeneous architectures. While the documents do not specify explicit protocols (e.g., OPC UA, BIM360), the framework supports **low-overhead updates** and **scalability** for integration with enterprise systems or other Digital Twins. Dependencies are managed through **modular service integration**, allowing custom services to be deployed independently without disrupting the core framework. The system bridges gaps between **BIM’s static representations** and dynamic operational needs in the AECO domain, emphasizing **interactive visualization** and **real-time monitoring** via BIM tools (e.g., Building Control Engineers’ use of BIM tools for device modeling)."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "In the context of **Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AECO)** Digital Twin (DT) systems, the provided documents do not explicitly detail **data governance policies, ownership frameworks, privacy protection mechanisms, or compliance with data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR, CCPA). However, the emphasis on **model-driven engineering (MDE)** in AECO DTs suggests a structured approach to **abstraction and reuse**, which could implicitly support modular data ownership (e.g., via domain-specific metamodels or ontologies) to enforce granular access controls. While **consent management** and **data anonymization** are not explicitly referenced, the reliance on **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** and **IoT platforms** implies integration with enterprise asset management systems that may inherently include privacy safeguards (e.g., role-based access control in proprietary ecosystems like Bentley’s iTwin.js). The lack of explicit mention of **data sharing agreements** or **anonymization techniques** leaves this characteristic as **Not in Document** for AECO-specific DTs, given the focus on **real-time data utilization** and **stakeholder coordination** without explicit governance layers. The absence of **Model-Driven Data Governance (MDE)** frameworks in surveyed works underscores a critical gap in formalizing ownership and privacy in complex AECO DTs."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **AECO-specific standards** and industry best practices for interoperability and compliance:

1. **Industry Standards**
   - **BIM Compatibility**: Aligns with **existing AECO systems and standards** (e.g., BIM) to ensure seamless integration with physical infrastructure models.
   - **Interoperability Standards**: Uses **standardized extension points** for modular, service-oriented DT platforms, enabling compatibility with third-party tools and protocols.

2. **Certification and Compliance**
   - **Not in Document**: No explicit mention of certification frameworks (e.g., ISO 19650, IFC, COBie) or formal compliance requirements beyond AECO-specific BIM standards.

3. **Technical Specifications**
   - **MDE Framework**: Leverages Model-Driven Engineering principles to automate DT artifact generation from enriched BIM models.
   - **Real-Time Protocols**: Implied support for low-latency data exchange (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets) for synchronized DT operations.

The framework prioritizes **unified semantic foundations** derived from BIM enrichment, ensuring interoperability with legacy and modern AECO systems.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents highlight **semantic drift** as a critical challenge in AECO Digital Twin systems, where **alignment between digital and physical models diverges over time**, necessitating **synchronization techniques** (e.g., rule-based management via declarative life-cycle specifications, as proposed by Kamburjan et al. [50]). This implies **runtime adaptability** and **safety-critical system considerations**, but **no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, authentication systems, or fail-safe mechanisms** is present. The focus on **BIM and IoT integration** (e.g., Opoku et al. [34]) suggests potential exposure to **IoT-specific vulnerabilities**, but no technical details on **threat mitigation strategies** or **risk assessment approaches** are provided. The reliance on **proprietary ecosystems** (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js) may inherently include some security safeguards, but their specifics are not detailed. **Ontological frameworks** (Oakes et al. [51]) for service integration imply **semantic-level security**, but no concrete protocols (e.g., OAuth, TLS) or safety-critical standards (e.g., ISO 26262 for automotive-grade DTs) are referenced. Thus, **security_and_safety_considerations** in AECO DTs are **Not in Document** beyond the abstract need for **synchronization and semantic coherence**."
]
